commit.message:
feat: RBAC system, authority engine, role-specific workbenches

RBAC System (Phase 1):
- 23 Entra ID roles covering carrier + MGA deployment types
- Full permission matrix from operating model spec (Section 3.1)
- 3-mode auth: dev (no auth), API key (demo), JWT (production-ready)
- CurrentUser with role checking and data scoping
- Carrier-only roles filtered in MGA deployments

Authority Engine:
- Configurable authority limits for quotes, binds, settlements, reserves
- Tiered escalation chains: agent → analyst → Sr UW → LOB Head → CUO
- AuthorityResult with decision (auto_execute/recommend/require_approval/escalate)
- Per-deployment configurable thresholds

Deployment Profiles:
- Carrier profile: all modules enabled (actuarial, reinsurance, MGA oversight)
- MGA profile: core modules only
- Module enable/disable via deployment_type config

Role-Specific Dashboards (Phase 2):
- Underwriter Workbench: split-view queue + tabbed detail + decision panel
- Claims Workbench: claim queue + assessment/timeline/financials + actions
- Compliance Workbench: AI inventory, decision audit, bias charts, override log
- Executive Dashboard: KPIs, premium/loss charts, pipeline, agent impact
- Broker Portal: external view with NO internal data exposure

Quality: 275 tests (76 new), lint clean, bandit clean, dashboard builds.

fix(ci): summary jobs fail when pipeline was skipped

All summary jobs had 'if: always()' which made skipped workflow runs
appear green. Now each summary checks if upstream jobs were skipped
and exits 1, so the overall run correctly shows as failed/red.

Also fixed cd-canary concurrency group to prevent workflow_run events
from cancelling workflow_dispatch runs.

refactor(mcp): granular lock splitting for BrowserState sub-groups

Move 7 sub-state groups (mobile, fleet, capture, network, workflow,
auth, ext) from BrowserState to independent Arc<Mutex<>> fields on
OneCrawlMcp. This eliminates contention between handlers that only
need sub-state access — they no longer block on the core browser lock.

BrowserState retains only the 8 core fields (session, page, tabs,
active_tab, pool, stealth_disabled, stealth_applied, headed).

Mixed-access sites (intercept_add_rule, intercept_remove_rule,
intercept_disable, context_transfer, session_info, orchestrator_devices)
use lock-extract-drop-lock pattern to avoid holding two locks
simultaneously.

Updated: state.rs, server.rs, 13 handler files, cdp_tools tests.
All 91 tests pass. Full workspace compiles cleanly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: move watchdog to atomic on-chain rescue v1 (breaking) (#17)

* feat(watchdog): migrate to atomic on-chain rescue v1

* add plans folder

* ci(contracts): run solidity tests with foundry

* fix(contracts): call setUserUseReserveAsCollateral on Pool, not DataProvider

On Aave v3 the DataProvider is read-only; setUserUseReserveAsCollateral
lives on the Pool contract. The previous code would revert on mainnet.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(watchdog): catch failed rescue tx to prevent retry flooding; add safety revert tests

Wrap live rescue submission in try/catch so a failed tx logs the error,
sets cooldown, and notifies instead of propagating and causing immediate
retries. Add Solidity tests for ResultingHFTooLow and AssetNotSupported
revert paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(watchdog): replace binary search with closed-form amount estimate

HF is linear in top-up amount, so two previewResultingHF calls (parallel)
plus one verification call suffice — down from ~26 sequential RPC calls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(contracts): remove _ensureCollateralEnabled — cannot toggle flag for another user

Aave v3's setUserUseReserveAsCollateral operates on msg.sender, so the
rescue contract was toggling the flag on its own position, not the user's.
Remove the broken call and document the precondition: the user must have
WBTC enabled as collateral before activating live rescue mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update plans/rescue-v1.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update plans/rescue-v1.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR #17 review comments

- Approve type(uint256).max in _forceApprove to avoid per-rescue re-approval
- Use toFixed(4) instead of toFixed(18) to avoid floating-point artifacts
- Add env overrides for WATCHDOG_DEADLINE_SECONDS and WATCHDOG_RESCUE_CONTRACT
- Fix misleading "HF is linear" comment to note it's an approximation
- Make MockPool HF increase proportional to supplied amount

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* revert: restore correct HF linearity comment and MockPool fixed increment

The reviewer incorrectly claimed HF is not linear in collateral amount.
In fact HF = Σ(collateral_i * LT_i) / totalDebt — the numerator is linear
in amount and totalDebt is constant, so HF IS exactly linear.

The MockPool `amount / 100` suggestion also broke tests: supplying
10_000_000 (0.1 WBTC) would add only 100_000 to an 18-decimal HF,
effectively zero. Restored the fixed 0.2e18 increment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(interpreter): optimize dispatch loop with cold/inline hints

  copy_reg) from #[inline] to #[inline(always)] to ensure they are
  always inlined into opcode handlers.

  Mov, Add, Sub, AddSmi, SubSmi, Inc, Dec, Return, Jump, JumpIfTrue,
  JumpIfFalse, JumpIfToBooleanTrue/False, LdaConstant, LdaUndefined,
  LdaNull, LdaTrue, LdaFalse, LdaTheHole) to help the compiler with
  code layout.

  error_message_from_value, stator_error_to_js_value, and new helpers
  bytecode_end_error, instruction_limit_error.

- Extract the dispatch error handler into a dedicated #[cold] function
  (handle_dispatch_error) to move exception-recovery code out of the
  hot loop, improving instruction cache locality.

- Add #[inline] to resolve_jump, constant_to_value and upgrade
  number_to_jsvalue to #[inline(always)].

* json: support reviver property deletion per ES §25.5.1.1

- Objects: properties whose reviver returns None are removed
- Arrays: elements whose reviver returns None become JsonValue::Null
- Top-level: None from the root reviver maps to JsonValue::Null

Add tests for both object property deletion and array element
replacement via the reviver.

fix: do not delete apt lists in cleanup stage

export-image/01-user-rename (pi-gen built-in) runs after our custom
stage and installs userconf-pi via 00-packages. Deleting
/var/lib/apt/lists/* in our 03-run-chroot.sh wiped the package index
so apt could not locate that package, causing:

  E: Unable to locate package userconf-pi
  Build failed

Fix: keep apt-get clean (removes .deb archives from cache, real size
saving) but leave the package lists intact so subsequent pi-gen stages
can install packages normally. The lists are small and compress well
inside the final .img.xz.

commit.message:
Fix monitoring startup in production via COMPOSE_FILE env var (#428)

* Initial plan

* Fix monitoring startup: add COMPOSE_FILE env var for easy monitoring activation

When users set LOCAL_MONITORING=true with PRODUCTION=true, Grafana and
Prometheus didn't start because they are defined in the monitoring overlay
file (docker-compose.monitoring.yml) but not in the base docker-compose.yml.

Users can now enable monitoring by adding two lines to their .env file:
  LOCAL_MONITORING=true
  COMPOSE_FILE=docker-compose.yml:docker-compose.monitoring.yml

Then just run: docker compose up -d

The traditional overlay syntax still works as before:
  docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.monitoring.yml up -d

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

* Move Grafana + Prometheus into the single container

Instead of running monitoring as separate Docker Compose services via an
overlay file, Prometheus and Grafana are now bundled directly in the
FiestaBoard container image. When LOCAL_MONITORING=true, the entrypoint
copies a supervisord snippet that starts both tools alongside the API,
Next.js, and nginx processes.

Key changes:
- Dockerfile: install Prometheus 2.53.4 and Grafana 12.4.0 binaries
- supervisord-monitoring.conf: new include for prometheus + grafana programs
- supervisord.conf/dev: add [include] for /app/conf.d/*.conf
- entrypoint.sh: conditionally enable monitoring on startup

commit.message:
Sprint 30: phase-filtered dispatch, DragSub, TerminalFocusSub, surrogate repair, option semaphore

Subscription phase contracts:
- MouseSub: now filtered to Pressed | Released only (was firing on all phases)
- ClickSub: now filtered to Pressed only (was firing on release and motion too)
- DragSub: new Sub case — fires only on Phase=Motion (Motion events were previously swallowed)
- TerminalFocusSub: new Sub case — fires on FocusGained/FocusLost terminal events (bool: gained)

Input parsing:
- parseEscape '[I' -> FocusGained, '[O' -> FocusLost (?1004h focus reporting)
- Detect.fs: enabled ?1004h in EnterRawMode/LeaveRawMode alongside ?1000h + ?1006h

Thread safety:
- eventSignal: null mutable -> SemaphoreSlim option (idiomatic F#, no null-deref before EnterRawMode)
- PollEvent: pattern-matches on Some/None instead of null check

Surrogate pair handling:
- High surrogate: reads low surrogate with 50ms timeout, reassembles via Rune.TryCreate
- Malformed/orphaned surrogates: emit space (was space anyway, but now documented correctly)
- Lone low surrogate: explicit case, emits space

Tea.fs:
- Sub.map: handles DragSub and TerminalFocusSub
- All mouse-related Sub cases have XML doc comments describing their phase contract

Tests (62 Phase 11 tests, 1,648 total):
- ClickSub: does not fire for Released; fires for Pressed
- MouseSub: does not fire for Motion; fires for Pressed and Released
- DragSub: fires only for Motion
- TerminalFocusSub: receives true (gained) and false (lost) in sequence
- Focus parsing: [I -> FocusGained, [O -> FocusLost

commit.message:
Add ethanol NPT example and fix 1-5 intra-molecular pair exclusions

Key changes:
- python/dmff_sr_custom_forces.py:
  * Add bond-force corrections for 1-5 (sep=4) intra-molecular pairs
    in both add_dmff_short_range_forces and add_damped_dispersion_force.
    Correction scale = mScale15 - 1, so net interaction = mScale15 × full.
    When mScale15=0: correction = -1 (subtracts nonbonded contribution
    that cannot be excluded due to CUDA MPIDForce Cov15 limitation).
  * Rename bonded_pairs → nb_pairs for clarity

- examples/water_mpid_npt/ff.backend_amoeba_total1000_classical_intra.xml:
  * Add ethanol (EA) intra-molecular bonded parameters:
    HarmonicBondForce (CT-CT, CT-HC, CT-OS, OS-HC),
    HarmonicAngleForce (5 angle types),
    PeriodicTorsionForce (HC-CT-CT-HC, HC-CT-CT-OS, CT-CT-OS-HC)
    with OPLS-AA parameters

- examples/ethanol_mpid_npt/ (new):
  * residues.xml: EA residue bond topology
  * ethanol_single.pdb: optimized single-molecule geometry
  * make_box.py: PACKMOL box generator (100 molecules, 21.5 Å)
  * ethanol_100mol.pdb: generated initial 100-molecule box
  * run_cuda_npt.py: NPT simulation script with temperature-ramp
    pre-equilibration (10K→50K→100K→200K→298K) to safely relax
    close inter-molecular contacts from the PACKMOL initial geometry

feat: Report management - history, export, invoice numbering, and tax settings (#14)

* feat: update frontend to version 0.1.1, add report history and viewer components

- Updated package version in package-lock.json to 0.1.1.
- Enhanced report model with new fields for contract time tracking.
- Implemented report service methods for fetching report history and exporting reports by ID.
- Created report history component with filtering and pagination features.
- Developed report viewer component to display detailed report information and export options.
- Added loading and error states to report viewer for better user experience.
- Updated routing to include new report history and viewer paths.
- Improved styling for report history and viewer components for better UI consistency.

* feat: Implement invoice number generation and management

- Added `invoice-number.service.ts` to handle invoice number generation with atomic updates.
- Integrated invoice number generation into the report generation process in `report.service.ts`.
- Updated `report.service.test.ts` to include tests for invoice number generation and uniqueness.
- Modified `report.model.ts` to include `invoiceNumber` in report summaries.
- Enhanced `user-settings.model.ts` to support invoice configuration settings.
- Updated user settings UI in `user-settings.component.ts` to allow configuration of invoice prefix and offset.
- Added invoice number display in report viewer and history components.
- Implemented delete functionality for reports in `report-history.component.ts` and `report-viewer.component.ts`.
- Updated navigation links to point to the report history page.
- Improved styling for invoice settings and report actions in respective components.

* feat: add tax settings to customer form and report generation

- Introduced tax settings in the customer model, including GST and PST options.
- Updated the customer form to allow configuration of tax settings with validation.
- Enhanced report generation to calculate and include tax amounts in the summary.
- Added tests for tax settings validation and form submission scenarios.

* feat: add bank settings to user settings and invoice PDF export

* chore: remove .opencode-workspace from tracking and add to .gitignore

* fix: address all PR #14 review comments

- Security: signed JWT download tokens (5-min expiry) verify file ownership
  before streaming; prevents cross-user file access
- Fix __dirname in report-export.service (use EXPORTS_DIR env or process.cwd())
- Wire deleteExportFile() on res finish/close to clean up export files
- Fix falsy zero-cost check in CSV (use !== undefined/null instead of truthy)
- Fix hardcoded 'USD' in CSV summary and PDF generateTotals() - resolve from contracts[0].currency
- Fix invoice number off-by-one: first invoice is now INV-1 (schema default 0 + $inc)
- Fix potential double /api in download URL when apiUrl already contains /api
- Remove dead exportReport() method and ReportOptions interface
- Wire getStatusClass() and formatDaysRemaining() helpers in report-viewer template
- Add takeUntilDestroyed(destroyRef) to all subscriptions in report-history and report-viewer

* fix: move download token from query string to X-Download-Token header

Replace ?token= query-string transport with the X-Download-Token HTTP
header on both sides so the token never appears in server access logs,
browser history, or Referer headers. The backend retains a query-string
fallback (?? operator) for a safe transition period. Adds new unit tests
for header-first, QS-fallback, no-token-401, and precedence scenarios.

* fix: address PR review comments - Date types and missing service tests

- Change Date to string for JSON-deserialized fields in ReportSummary and ReportListItem
  (generationDate, startDate, endDate, period.startDate, period.endDate)
- Add 8 unit tests for new ReportService methods (getReportHistory, getReportById,
  exportReportById, deleteReport) using HttpTestingController

Addresses Copilot review comments r2902160909 and r2902160914

* fix: correct nextInvoiceNumber initial value expectation to 0 in integration tests

* fix: address Copilot PR review comments

- auth: add purpose:'download' claim to download tokens to prevent
  replay attacks as Bearer tokens (r2902233156)
- report.controller: remove query-string token fallback, header-only
